message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Code ready for HTMX review, pointing to files">
Carson, the code is already committed and pushed with CI green. Here are the key files for your HTMX review:

**Templates:**
- `templates/next_actions.html` — full page with context filter pills, add form, action list
- `templates/next_action_item.html` — individual item with complete/edit/delete (hx-post, hx-swap)
- `templates/next_action_edit.html` — inline edit form

**Routes (HTMX branching):**
- `src/routes/next_actions.rs` — all 7 handlers use `is_htmx_request()` branching, `htmx_response_with_announce()` for announcements

**Key patterns implemented:**
1. Context filtering uses plain `<a>` links with `?context=UUID` query params — works without JS, hx-boost handles enhancement
2. Complete/delete use `hx-post` with `hx-target="closest .next-action-item"` and `hx-swap="outerHTML"` — empty response removes the item
3. Edit uses `hx-get` to swap item with edit form, `hx-post` to save, cancel link uses `hx-get` to restore the item view
4. Add form uses `hx-post` targeting the list, `hx-swap="beforeend"` to append new items
5. All non-HTMX paths fall back to `Redirect::to("/next-actions")`
6. All mutations use `htmx_response_with_announce()` for aria-live

Please review and share any blocking or non-blocking feedback.
</teammate-message>

content.text: I've already completed my review and sent it to you. Let me re-send the summary in case the previous message didn't come through clearly.

input.content:
Luca, I already sent my full review — please check your earlier messages. But here's the quick summary: **BLOCKING (1):** Context filter links (`next_actions.html` lines 18-28) rely on hx-boost for full-page reload. Context switching is the *primary* interaction on this page — the user story says "find what I can do right now based on where I am" within 10 seconds. Full-page reloads for every filter click (nav, header, add form all re-render) is noticeably worse than partial swap. Fix: Wrap the empty state + list in `
`, then add explicit HTMX attributes on the filter links: ```html {{ ctx.name }} ``` The plain `href` stays as the no-JS fallback. No backend changes needed — the server already returns the full page; `hx-select` pulls out just the content div. Also need to update the filter pills' active state — the `aria-current` on the clicked link needs to update. The `hx-select` approach will handle this naturally if you include the filter nav in the target wrapper, or you could target a broader wrapper. Either way, the filter needs to feel instant. **Minor (1):** Cancel link in `next_action_edit.html` (line 22) lacks `aria-label="Cancel editing {{ action.title }}"` — inconsistent with `context_edit.html` which has it. **Nice-to-have (3, not blocking):** - Add form intentionally doesn't use `this.reset()` to preserve the select value — good UX, add a comment explaining why - Consider `hx-confirm` on delete for next actions (more invested effort than inbox items) - Empty state when no contexts exist should mention contexts are needed + link to `/contexts` Everything else is excellent — progressive enhancement, `htmx_response_with_announce()`, locality of behavior, edit-in-place pattern, URL structure all match established conventions.
input.summary: Re-sent review summary: 1 BLOCKING (filter partial swap), 1 minor, 3 nice-to-have

content.text: Review re-sent. I'm waiting for Luca to address the blocking item (context filter partial swap) before I can give consensus approval.
